name: fim-one
description: LLM-powered Agent Runtime with Dynamic DAG Planning & Concurrent Execution

commit.message:
chore: update banner images

- Replace banner.jpg and banner.png in assets/ and docs/

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: update Twitter/X and Product Hunt URLs across docs and READMEs

- Correct Twitter/X handle casing from fim_one to FIM_One

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: complete fim-agent to fim-one rename across entire codebase

- Source code: update __fim_origin__ URLs, User-Agent strings, X-Powered-By header, docstrings, comments
- Documentation: README.md, README.zh.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, LICENSE, all docs/*.mdx and docs/zh/*.mdx

- Examples: quickstart.py references

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: rename package from fim_agent to fim_one

- Rename Python package directory src/fim_agent/ -> src/fim_one/
- Update all Python imports across source and tests from fim_agent to fim_one
- Update pyproject.toml package name (fim-agent -> fim-one) and license ref
- Update docker-compose.yml service name and comments
- Update alembic.ini, start.sh, Dockerfile, docker-entrypoint.sh references
- Update all documentation (EN + ZH) to reflect new naming
- Fix README/README.zh TOC anchors (#why-fim-agent -> #why-fim-one)
- Update example.env, examples/quickstart.py, frontend constants
- Update CONTRIBUTING.md, LICENSE, issue templates, and uv.lock

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

style(tools): improve tool card description layout and update connector copy

- Add min-h-[2.5rem] to ToolCard, ConnectorLinkCard, and MCPLinkCard description paragraphs for consistent card heights
- Change line-clamp-3 to line-clamp-2 on ConnectorLinkCard and MCPLinkCard for visual uniformity
- Update connector description copy (EN): broaden from "HTTP API actions" to "Custom actions (APIs, databases, etc.)"
- Update connector description copy (ZH): match EN change with "自定义操作（API、数据库等）"
- Add agents-page.png screenshot asset

🤖 Generated with Claude Code

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(eval): add ReAct mode badge, stats overview panel, and latency/distribution cards

- ReAct execution mode badge with tooltip on results page
- "Execution mode: ReAct" notice on start eval dialog
- Stats panel: pass/fail/error counts, min/avg/max latency, total tokens
- Stacked distribution bar (green/red/orange)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: add Evaluation Center documentation page (EN + ZH)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(eval): use empty string routes instead of "/" to avoid 307 redirect dropping auth header

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: lower structured_llm_call fallback logs to DEBUG, update docs

The native_fc → 400 → json_mode fallback is expected behavior for
Anthropic thinking models, not an error. Demote both log lines from
WARNING/INFO to DEBUG so they don't alarm users in normal operation.

Updated troubleshooting section in llm-provider-guide (EN + ZH) to
clarify this is expected, costs ~300ms and zero tokens.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: revert is_anthropic_thinking — keep tool_call=True unconditionally

The 400 from structured_llm_call's forced tool_choice + thinking is
handled by existing try/except fallback. The log noise (INFO level) is
acceptable; breaking ReAct's _run_native is not.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(types): align agentApi.publish scope type with backend — add "personal"

- api.ts: extend scope union from "org"|"global" to "personal"|"org"|"global"
- agents/page.tsx: remove now-unnecessary `as` cast that was masking the mismatch

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Organizations, KB AI Panel, shared AIPanel refactor, DB schema UX improvements

- Add shared AIPanel component extracted from AgentAIPanel and AIActionPanel (DRY refactor)
- Add KBAIPanel for knowledge base AI assistant (new tab on KB detail page)
- Add kb-ai-panel.tsx and ai-panel.tsx as new shared components
- Add agent publish scope selector (personal vs org) with org picker in agents page
- Add connector type label in agent settings form (Database vs API · N actions)
- Add syncing/synced/syncFailed i18n keys to common.json (EN + ZH)
- Add aiAnnotateAllLoading/Hint/batches keys to connectors.json for batch annotation UX
- Add AI assistant tab and i18n keys to KB detail page (kb.json EN + ZH)
- Add orgCreated/orgDeleted/leftOrganization/deleteOrgDescription/leaveOrgDescription/removeMemberDescription/membersSheetTitle/roleOwner keys to organizations.json (EN + ZH)
- Fix DB connector page: use forceMount + data-[state=inactive]:hidden on all TabsContent to prevent unmount flicker
- Fix SchemaManager: add refreshKey prop; only mount Schema/Query tabs when isDatabase
- Fix Turbopack root config in next.config.ts
- Reorder Settings page tabs: organizations before appearance
- Add emoji picker to admin-organizations create/edit dialogs; show org icon in table
- Add onSchemaChanged callback from AIActionPanel to parent connector page
- Add extension-guide.mdx (EN + ZH) docs
- Update roadmap: add DB Schema Advanced Builder and KB Advanced Editor items (EN + ZH)
- Backend: update agent/connector/db_connector/KB/organization API and schemas for org-scoped publish, schema sync callbacks, and related endpoints

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: add LLM Provider Compatibility guide (EN + ZH)

New architecture doc covering provider detection, tool_choice modes,
the Bedrock prefill trap, and Anthropic thinking + forced tool_choice
incompatibility. Includes quick-reference compatibility matrix and
troubleshooting section. Added to Execution Engines nav group.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(eval): fix JSON syntax error in zh/eval.json — escape double quotes in assertionPlaceholder

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: restore tool_call=True — is_anthropic_thinking was breaking ReAct native mode

The is_anthropic_thinking guard (added in aa1eaa0) set tool_call=False for
Anthropic models with extended thinking. Intent: skip forced tool_choice in
structured_llm_call (which Anthropic rejects with thinking enabled).

Unintended side effect: ReAct's _run_native uses tool_choice="auto" which
works fine with thinking — but the False ability flag forced it into _run_json
→ response_format=json_object → LiteLLM prefill injection → Bedrock 400.

Fix: abilities.tool_call is now always True. structured_llm_call already has
try/except that catches the forced-tool_choice 400 and falls through to
json_mode/plain_text gracefully — no abilities-level intervention needed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: update changelog and roadmap for Evaluation Center feature

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(eval): add Evaluation Center frontend — types, API client, pages, i18n, nav item

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(eval): add Evaluation Center backend — ORM models, migration, dataset+case CRUD, run execution with async grading

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: update changelog with json_mode_enabled and admin form UX (EN + ZH)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add json_mode_enabled per-model flag to fix Bedrock assistant prefill rejection

Root cause: LiteLLM injects an assistant prefill message {role:"assistant",content:"{"}
when response_format=json_object is used with anthropic/ models. Newer AWS Bedrock versions
reject conversations ending with an assistant message (ValidationException on 2nd+ iteration).

Changes:
- Add json_mode_enabled bool field to ModelConfig ORM + Alembic migration (default TRUE)
- OpenAICompatibleLLM: replace is_bedrock/is_anthropic_relay heuristics with explicit
  _json_mode_enabled param — abilities.json_mode now reads this directly (no URL guessing)
- deps.py: wire json_mode_enabled from DB config; add LLM_JSON_MODE_ENABLED env var for
  ENV-only deployments (default true; set false for Bedrock relays)
- admin-models.tsx: restructure form — only Name/Model Name/API Key visible by default;
  all other settings (Base URL, Provider, Temperature, Tokens, JSON Mode) in collapsible
  Advanced section (collapsed by default); add JSON Mode toggle with description
- Sync i18n keys (EN + ZH), example.env, and docs/configuration/environment-variables.mdx

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add missing i18n keys for organization-settings component (EN + ZH)

44 keys used by settings component were missing from both locales.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: unwrap ApiResponse.data in org API methods — members.map TypeError

listOrgMembers, orgApi.list/create/update/listMembers/changeRole all returned
raw { data: ... } wrapper; .then(r => r.data) added to each.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add Organizations tab to user settings — create/join/manage orgs

- Add "Organizations" tab to settings page with Building2 icon
- New OrganizationSettings component: list orgs, create/edit/delete/leave
- OrgCard with role badges (owner=purple, admin=blue, member=gray), DropdownMenu actions
- OrgFormDialog: name, auto-generated slug, description, emoji icon; dirty-state protection
- MembersSheet: list members with role badges, add member form, change role, remove member
- orgApi in api.ts: list/create/update/delete orgs, list/add/change-role/remove members

docs: update changelog with column visibility toggle and AI no-op detection (EN + ZH)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(db-connector): immediate column visibility toggle with optimistic update

- Add handleToggleColumnVisible with optimistic UI + rollback on error
- Replace inline updateColumnField with immediate save + toast feedback
- Add columnNowVisible/columnNowHidden i18n keys (EN + ZH)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(agent-ai): skip update callback when AI refinement produces no actual changes

- Backend: compare old vs new value before setattr, skip identical fields
- Frontend: check modified_fields length, show info toast instead of success when nothing changed

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(db-connector): real LLM-powered AI schema annotation + UX fixes

Backend:
- Replace _humanize_name() stub with real LLM call via get_fast_llm()
- Structured prompt asks for Chinese display_name + description for
  tables and columns, with context-aware annotation rules
- Robust JSON parser handles code fences, wrapper objects, bare arrays
- Graceful fallback to _humanize_name() if LLM call fails
- Accept table_ids (UUIDs) in addition to table_names in request

Frontend:
- Add "AI Annotate All" button in schema manager left panel header
- Keep per-table AI Annotate button in right panel detail view
- Sync connector detail tab state to URL ?tab= query param
- Add i18n keys for aiAnnotateAll / aiAnnotateAllSuccess (EN + ZH)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(db-connector): preserve password on update, driver fallback, required fields

Three fixes for DB connector settings:

1. Password sentinel: when update sends password "***" (masked),
   preserve the existing encrypted_password instead of encrypting
   the literal "***". Prevents real password loss on save.

2. Driver fallback: load driver with || "postgresql" fallback to
   prevent undefined state when backend response lacks the key.

3. Required fields: add * markers and required attributes to
   username and port fields; add username to submit/save guards.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(db-connector): resolve masked password on ad-hoc test connection

When a saved connector's password is displayed as "***" (masked by
the backend), clicking Test Connection would send the literal "***"
and fail. Now the ad-hoc endpoint accepts an optional connector_id;
when password is "***" and connector_id is provided, the real
password is decrypted from the saved config on the server side.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(db-connector): test connection always uses current form values

Always use the ad-hoc /test-connection endpoint with current form
values instead of the saved-connector endpoint. This ensures the
test reflects what's on screen, not the last-saved config.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: update changelog for scrolling fix and test connection UX (EN + ZH)

fix(migration): make base_url nullable on SQLite via batch_alter_table

c1d2e3f4 incorrectly assumed SQLite doesn't enforce NOT NULL — it does.
Added batch_alter_table for SQLite in the original migration (for fresh
installs) and a fixup migration 376d120aac52 for existing DBs.

feat(memory): track compacted message IDs and filter empty assistant messages

- Add `compacted_message_ids` field to DbMemory for downstream consumers to know which messages were dropped during compaction
- Filter out empty assistant messages (native FC tool-calling intermediates with no content) before compaction to improve history quality
- Map ChatMessage objects to their DB row IDs to accurately track which messages survive truncation/compaction

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(db-connector): fix asyncpg execute_query row limit bug, add Phase 4 to roadmap

asyncpg's PreparedStatement.fetch(n) treats n as a bind parameter,
not a row count limit. Replaced with cursor iteration to correctly
cap returned rows. Also added Phase 4 enterprise drivers to roadmap.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(migration): use sa.text("(CURRENT_TIMESTAMP)") for SQLite/PG compatibility

feat(db-connector): add backend API routes for DB connector management

Add Pydantic schemas for database connector config, test-connection,
introspection, schema management, query execution, and AI annotation.
Add API endpoints: POST test-connection, POST introspect (auto-discover

schemas, POST query (with SQL safety validation), and POST ai/annotate
(basic name humanization stub).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: remove duplicate get_user_org_ids + fix SQLite boolean literal in migration

- Remove dead Wave 1 get_user_org_ids that queried non-existent `org_members` table
- Fix migration backfill: use parameterized `is_global = :val` instead of raw `TRUE` literal (SQLite compat)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(db-connector): add DatabaseToolAdapter (3 tools per connector)

Create DatabaseToolAdapter that produces list_tables, describe_table,
and query tools per database connector. Tools use cached schema info
for metadata and ConnectionPoolManager for live queries. The query
tool embeds schema context in its description so the LLM can write
accurate SQL, validates statements via SqlSafetyValidator, and logs
all calls through the existing connector call log callback.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(db-connector): add database driver abstraction with PG and MySQL drivers

Add DatabaseDriver ABC with connect/disconnect/test/list_tables/
describe_table/execute_query interface. Implement PostgreSQLDriver
(asyncpg) and MySQLDriver (aiomysql) with connection pooling, schema
introspection via information_schema, query timeout enforcement, and
automatic row serialization. Add ConnectionPoolManager singleton with
LRU eviction for per-connector driver caching.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Merge branch 'worktree-agent-acd1322e'
Merge branch 'worktree-agent-ab9a2b75'

feat(db-connector): add encryption module and SQL safety validation

Add Fernet field-level encryption derived from JWT_SECRET_KEY for
encrypting database passwords at rest. Add SQL safety validator that
blocks multi-statement queries, dangerous patterns (SLEEP, xp_cmdshell,
LOAD_FILE, etc.), and restricts to SELECT-only unless writes are
explicitly permitted.

database_schemas and schema_columns tables, add db_config JSON column
to connectors, and make base_url nullable. Add DatabaseSchema and

feat: add organization admin UI (Wave 1 frontend) — admin-organizations component, i18n

- Create AdminOrganizations component following admin-users.tsx patterns:
  paginated table, DropdownMenu row actions, create/edit dialogs, delete
  AlertDialog, member management Sheet with add/remove/change-role

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use literal_column instead of literal for PG coalesce GROUP BY

sa.literal() still generates bind parameters with asyncpg driver.
literal_column("'Unknown'") embeds raw SQL, and using a shared variable
ensures SELECT and GROUP BY reference the exact same expression.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: update changelog for admin stats PG GroupingError fix (EN + ZH)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add organization system (Wave 1 backend) — ORM models, migration, auth helpers, org API

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use literal() in coalesce GROUP BY to fix PG GroupingError on admin stats

PostgreSQL treats separate bind parameters ($1, $2) as potentially different
values, so GROUP BY coalesce(col, $2) doesn't match SELECT coalesce(col, $1).
Using sa.literal() embeds the value inline, fixing the mismatch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: change let to const for uploadedFiles array (prefer-const lint error)

docs: sync changelog and Chinese translations for DooD sandbox fix

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(docker): resolve DooD sandbox volume mount path mismatch

When fim-agent runs inside a container (docker-compose) with
CODE_EXEC_BACKEND=docker, sandbox scripts were written to container-
internal paths (/app/src/tmp/...) invisible to the host Docker daemon,
causing empty -v mounts and "No such file or directory" errors.

Three fixes:
1. Move sandbox dir from tmp/conversations/ to data/sandbox/ (under
   the already-mounted ./data:/app/data volume) and use
   _PROJECT_ROOT.parent to resolve the correct /app/data/ path
   (parents[3] yields /app/src/, not /app/)
2. Add DOCKER_HOST_DATA_DIR env var + _resolve_host_path() to translate
   container paths → host paths for -v bind mounts; docker-compose.yml
   auto-sets via ${PWD}/data (absolute)
3. Handle empty-string env var edge case (or None)

readme_html:
🌐 English | 🇨🇳 中文

AI-Powered Connector Hub — embed into one system as a Copilot, or connect them all as a Hub.

🌐 Website · 📖 Docs · 📋 Changelog · 🐛 Report Bug · 💬 Discord · 🐦 Twitter · 🏆 Product Hunt

Table of Contents

  • Overview
  • Use Cases
  • Why FIM One
  • Where FIM One Sits
  • Key Features
  • Architecture
  • Quick Start (Docker / Local / Production)
  • Configuration
  • Development
  • Roadmap
  • Contributing
  • Star History
  • Activity
  • Contributors
  • License

Overview

FIM One is a provider-agnostic Python framework for building AI agents that dynamically plan and execute complex tasks. What makes it different is the Connector Hub architecture — three delivery modes, one agent core:

Mode What it is How you access it
Standalone General-purpose AI assistant — search, code, knowledge base Portal
Copilot AI embedded in a host system — works alongside users in their existing UI iframe / widget / embed into host pages
Hub Central AI orchestration — all your systems connected, cross-system intelligence Portal / API 
graph LR
    ERP --> Hub["FIM One Hub<br/>(AI orchestration)"]
    Database --> Hub
    Lark --> Hub
    CRM --> Hub
    OA --> Hub
    API[Custom API] --> Hub

The core is always the same: ReAct reasoning loops, dynamic DAG planning with concurrent execution, pluggable tools, and a protocol-first architecture with zero vendor lock-in.

Use Cases

Enterprise data and workflows are locked inside OA, ERP, finance, and approval systems. FIM One lets AI agents read and write those systems — automating cross-system processes without modifying your existing infrastructure.

Scenario Recommended Start What it automates
Legal & Compliance Copilot → Hub Contract clause extraction, version diff, risk flagging with source citations, auto-trigger OA approval
IT Operations Hub Alert fires → logs pulled → root cause analyzed → fix dispatched to Lark/Slack — one closed loop
Business Operations Copilot Scheduled data summaries pushed to team channels; ad-hoc natural language queries against live databases
Finance Automation Hub Invoice verification, expense approval routing, ledger reconciliation across ERP and accounting systems
Procurement Copilot → Hub Requirements → vendor comparison → contract draft → approval — Agent handles the cross-system handoffs
Developer Integration API Import an OpenAPI spec or describe an API in chat — connector created in minutes, auto-registered as agent tools

Why FIM One

Land and Expand

Start by embedding a Copilot into one system — say, your ERP. Users interact with AI right inside their familiar interface: query financial data, generate reports, get answers without leaving the page.

When the value is proven, set up a Hub — a central portal that connects all your systems together. The ERP Copilot keeps running embedded; the Hub adds cross-system orchestration: query contracts in CRM, check approvals in OA, notify stakeholders on Lark — all from one place.

Copilot proves value inside one system. Hub unlocks value across all systems.

What FIM One Does NOT Do

FIM One does not replicate workflow logic that already exists in your target systems:

  • No BPM/FSM engine — Approval chains, routing, escalation, and state machines are the target system's responsibility. These systems spent years building this logic.
  • No drag-and-drop workflow editor — Use Dify if you need visual flowcharts. FIM One's DAG planner generates execution graphs dynamically.
  • Connector = API call — From the connector's perspective, "transfer approval" = one API call, "reject with reason" = one API call. All complex workflow operations collapse to HTTP requests. FIM One calls the API; the target system manages the state.

This is a deliberate architectural boundary, not a capability gap.

Competitive Positioning

Dify Manus Coze FIM One
Approach Visual workflow builder Autonomous agent Builder + agent space AI Connector Hub
Planning Human-designed static DAGs Multi-agent CoT Static + dynamic LLM DAG planning + ReAct
Cross-system API nodes (manual) No Plugin marketplace Hub Mode (N:N orchestration)
Human Confirmation No No No Yes (pre-execution gate)
Self-hosted Yes (Docker stack) No Yes (Coze Studio) Yes (single process)

Deep dive: Philosophy | Execution Modes | Competitive Landscape

Where FIM One Sits

                Static Execution          Dynamic Execution
            ┌──────────────────────┬──────────────────────┐
 Static     │ BPM / Workflow       │ ACM                  │
 Planning   │ Camunda, Activiti    │ (Salesforce Case)    │
            │ Dify, n8n, Coze     │                      │
            ├──────────────────────┼──────────────────────┤
 Dynamic    │ (transitional —      │ Autonomous Agent     │
 Planning   │  unstable quadrant)  │ AutoGPT, Manus       │
            │                      │ ★ FIM One (bounded)│
            └──────────────────────┴──────────────────────┘

Dify/n8n are Static Planning + Static Execution — humans design the DAG on a visual canvas, nodes execute fixed operations. FIM One is Dynamic Planning + Dynamic Execution — LLM generates the DAG at runtime, each node runs a ReAct loop, with re-planning when goals aren't met. But bounded (max 3 re-plan rounds, token budgets, confirmation gates), so more controlled than AutoGPT.

FIM One doesn't do BPM/FSM — workflow logic belongs to the target system, Connectors just call APIs.

Full explanation: Philosophy

Key Features

Connector Platform (the core)

  • Connector Hub Architecture — Standalone assistant, embedded Copilot, or central Hub — same agent core, different delivery.
  • Any System, One Pattern — Connect APIs, databases, and message buses. Actions auto-register as agent tools with auth injection (Bearer, API Key, Basic).
  • Database Connectors — Direct SQL access to PostgreSQL, MySQL, Oracle, SQL Server, and Chinese legacy databases (DM, KingbaseES, GBase, Highgo). Schema introspection, AI-powered annotation, read-only query execution, and encrypted credentials at rest. Each DB connector auto-generates 3 tools (list_tables, describe_table, query).
  • Three Ways to Build Connectors:
    • Import OpenAPI spec — upload YAML/JSON/URL; connectors and all actions generated automatically.
    • AI chat builder — describe the API in natural language; AI generates and iterates the action config in-conversation. 10 specialized builder tools handle connector settings, actions, testing, and agent wiring.
    • MCP ecosystem — connect any MCP server directly; the third-party MCP community works out of the box.

Intelligent Planning & Execution

  • Dynamic DAG Planning — LLM decomposes goals into dependency graphs at runtime. No hard-coded workflows.
  • Concurrent Execution — Independent steps run in parallel via asyncio.
  • DAG Re-Planning — Auto-revises the plan up to 3 rounds when goals aren't met.
  • ReAct Agent — Structured reasoning-and-acting loop with automatic error recovery.
  • Extended Thinking — Enable chain-of-thought reasoning for supported models (OpenAI o-series, Gemini 2.5+, Claude) via LLM_REASONING_EFFORT. The model's reasoning is surfaced in the UI "thinking" step.

Tools & Integrations

  • Pluggable Tool System — Auto-discovery; ships with Python executor, Node.js executor, calculator, web search/fetch, HTTP request, shell exec, and more.
  • Pluggable Sandbox — python_exec / node_exec / shell_exec run in local or Docker mode (CODE_EXEC_BACKEND=docker) for OS-level isolation (--network=none, --memory=256m). Safe for SaaS and multi-tenant deployments.
  • MCP Protocol — Connect any MCP server as tools. Third-party MCP ecosystem works out of the box.
  • Tool Artifact System — Tools produce rich outputs (HTML previews, generated files) with in-chat rendering and download. HTML artifacts render in sandboxed iframes; file artifacts show download chips.
  • OpenAI-Compatible — Works with any /v1/chat/completions provider (OpenAI, DeepSeek, Qwen, Ollama, vLLM…).

RAG & Knowledge

  • Full RAG Pipeline — Jina embedding + LanceDB + FTS + RRF hybrid retrieval + reranker. Supports PDF, DOCX, Markdown, HTML, CSV.
  • Grounded Generation — Evidence-anchored RAG with inline [N] citations, conflict detection, and explainable confidence scores.
  • KB Document Management — Chunk-level CRUD, text search across chunks, failed document retry, and auto-migrating vector store schema.

Portal & UX

  • Real-time Streaming (SSE v2) — Split event protocol (done / suggestions / title / end) with streaming dot-pulse cursor, KaTeX math rendering, and tool step folding.
  • DAG Visualization — Interactive flow graph with live status, dependency edges, click-to-scroll, and re-plan round snapshots as collapsible cards.
  • Conversational Interrupt — Send follow-up messages while the agent is running; injected at the next iteration boundary.
  • Dark / Light / System Theme — Full theme support with system-preference detection.
  • Command Palette — Conversation search, starring, batch operations, and title rename.

Platform & Multi-Tenant

  • JWT Auth — Token-based SSE auth, conversation ownership, per-user resource isolation.
  • Agent Management — Create, configure, and publish agents with bound models, tools, and instructions. Per-agent execution mode (Standard/Planner) and temperature control.
  • Admin Panel — System stats dashboard (users, conversations, tokens, model usage charts, tokens-by-agent breakdown), connector call metrics (success rate, latency, call counts), user management with search/pagination, role toggle, password reset, account enable/disable, and per-tool enable/disable controls.
  • First-Run Setup Wizard — On first launch, the portal guides you through creating an admin account (username, password, email). This one-time setup becomes your login credential — no config files needed.
  • Personal Center — Per-user global system instructions, applied across all conversations.
  • Language Preference — Per-user language setting (auto/en/zh) that directs all LLM responses to the chosen language.

Context & Memory

  • LLM Compact — Automatic LLM-powered summarization to stay within token budgets.
  • ContextGuard + Pinned Messages — Token budget manager; pinned messages are protected from compaction.
  • Dual Database Support — SQLite (zero-config default) for getting started in seconds; PostgreSQL for production and multi-worker deployments. Docker Compose auto-provisions PostgreSQL with health checks. docker compose up and you're live.

Architecture

System Overview

graph TB
    subgraph app["Application & Interaction Layer"]
        a["Portal · API · iframe · Lark/Slack Bot · Webhook · WeCom/DingTalk"]
    end
    subgraph mid["FIM One Middleware"]
        direction LR
        m1["Connectors<br/>+ MCP Hub"] ~~~ m2["Orch Engine<br/>ReAct / DAG"] ~~~ m3["RAG /<br/>Knowledge"] ~~~ m4["Auth /<br/>Admin"]
    end
    subgraph biz["Business Systems & Data Layer"]
        b["ERP · CRM · OA · Finance · Databases · Custom APIs<br/>Lark · DingTalk · WeCom · Slack · Email · Webhook"]
    end
    app --> mid --> biz

Connector Hub

graph LR
    ERP["ERP<br/>(SAP/Kingdee)"] --> A
    CRM["CRM<br/>(Salesforce)"] --> B
    OA["OA<br/>(Seeyon/Weaver)"] --> C
    DB["Custom DB<br/>(PG/MySQL)"] --> D
    subgraph Hub["FIM One Hub"]
        A["Agent A: Finance Audit"]
        B["Agent B: Contract Review"]
        C["Agent C: Approval Assist"]
        D["Agent D: Data Reporting"]
    end
    A --> O1["Lark / Slack"]
    B --> O2["Email / WeCom"]
    C --> O3["Teams / Webhook"]
    D --> O4["Any API"]

Portal / API / iframe

Each connector is a standardized bridge — the agent doesn't know or care whether it's talking to SAP or a custom PostgreSQL database. See Connector Architecture for details.

Internal Execution

FIM One provides two execution modes:

Mode Best for How it works
ReAct Single complex queries Reason → Act → Observe loop with tools
DAG Planning Multi-step parallel tasks LLM generates dependency graph, independent steps run concurrently 
graph TB
    Q[User Query] --> P["DAG Planner<br/>LLM decomposes the goal into steps + dependency edges"]
    P --> E["DAG Executor<br/>Launches independent steps concurrently via asyncio<br/>Each step is handled by a ReAct Agent"]
    E --> R1["ReAct Agent 1 → Tools<br/>(python_exec, custom, ...)"]
    E --> R2["ReAct Agent 2 → RAG<br/>(retriever interface)"]
    E --> RN["ReAct Agent N → ..."]
    R1 & R2 & RN --> An["Plan Analyzer<br/>LLM evaluates results · re-plans if goal not met"]
    An --> F[Final Answer]

Quick Start

Option A: Docker (recommended)

No local Python or Node.js required — everything is built inside the container.

git clone https://github.com/fim-ai/fim-one.git
cd fim-one

# Configure — only LLM_API_KEY is required
cp example.env .env
# Edit .env: set LLM_API_KEY (and optionally LLM_BASE_URL, LLM_MODEL)

# Build and run (first time, or after pulling new code)
docker compose up --build -d

Open http://localhost:3000 — on first launch you'll be guided through creating an admin account. That's it.

After the initial build, subsequent starts only need:

docker compose up -d          # start (skip rebuild if image unchanged)
docker compose down           # stop
docker compose logs -f        # view logs

Data is persisted in Docker named volumes (fim-data, fim-uploads) and survives container restarts.

Note: Docker mode does not support hot reload. Code changes require rebuilding the image (docker compose up --build -d). For active development with live reload, use Option B below.

Option B: Local Development

Prerequisites: Python 3.11+, uv, Node.js 18+, pnpm.

git clone https://github.com/fim-ai/fim-one.git
cd fim-one

cp example.env .env
# Edit .env: set LLM_API_KEY

# Install
uv sync --all-extras
cd frontend && pnpm install && cd ..

# Launch (with hot reload)
./start.sh dev
Command What starts URL
./start.sh Next.js + FastAPI http://localhost:3000 (UI) + :8000 (API)
./start.sh dev Same, with hot reload (Python --reload + Next.js HMR) Same
./start.sh api FastAPI only (headless, for integration or testing) http://localhost:8000/api

Production Deployment

Both options work in production:

Method Command Best for
Docker docker compose up -d Hands-off deployment, easy updates
Script ./start.sh Bare-metal servers, custom process managers

For either method, put an Nginx reverse proxy in front for HTTPS and custom domain:

User → Nginx (443/HTTPS) → localhost:3000

The API runs internally on port 8000 — Next.js proxies /api/* requests automatically. Only port 3000 needs to be exposed.

If you use the code execution sandbox (CODE_EXEC_BACKEND=docker), mount the Docker socket:

# docker-compose.yml
volumes:
  - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock

Configuration

Recommended Setup

FIM One works with any OpenAI-compatible LLM provider — OpenAI, DeepSeek, Anthropic, Qwen, Ollama, vLLM, and more. Pick whichever you prefer:

Provider LLM_API_KEY LLM_BASE_URL LLM_MODEL
OpenAI sk-... (default) gpt-4o
DeepSeek sk-... https://api.deepseek.com/v1 deepseek-chat
Anthropic sk-ant-... https://api.anthropic.com/v1 claude-sonnet-4-6
Ollama (local) ollama http://localhost:11434/v1 qwen2.5:14b

Jina AI unlocks web search/fetch, embedding, and the full RAG pipeline (free tier available).

Minimal .env:

LLM_API_KEY=sk-your-key
# LLM_BASE_URL=https://api.openai.com/v1   # default — change for other providers
# LLM_MODEL=gpt-4o                         # default — change for other models

JINA_API_KEY=jina_...                       # unlocks web tools + RAG

All Variables

See the full Environment Variables reference for all configuration options (LLM, agent execution, web tools, RAG, code execution, image generation, connectors, platform, OAuth).

Development

# Install all dependencies (including dev extras)
uv sync --all-extras

# Run tests
pytest

# Run tests with coverage
pytest --cov=fim_one --cov-report=term-missing

# Lint
ruff check src/ tests/

# Type check
mypy src/

Roadmap

See the full Roadmap for version history and what's next.

Contributing

We welcome contributions of all kinds — code, docs, translations, bug reports, and ideas.

Pioneer Program: The first 100 contributors who get a PR merged are recognized as Founding Contributors with permanent credits in the project, a badge on their profile, and priority issue support. Learn more →

Quick links:

  • Contributing Guide — setup, conventions, PR process
  • Good First Issues — curated for newcomers
  • Open Issues — bugs & feature requests
